$comment: Protocol-level test mappings for requirements not provable by fixtures alone

mappings.test_description: Steps MUST be performed in specified order
mappings.notes: Validation order proven by step-precedence assertions

mappings.test_description: Hard error MUST terminate validation immediately
mappings.notes: Step 1 signature fail before Step 4 schema parse

mappings.test_description: Verifier MUST NOT continue after hard error
mappings.notes: No subsequent step effects observable after hard error

mappings.test_description: Step 3 kernel constraints fail before Step 7 issuer binding
mappings.notes: Constraint violation takes precedence over issuer mismatch

mappings.test_description: Strict profile enforces typ presence
mappings.notes: Existing test coverage in verify-local.test.ts

mappings.test_description: Strict profile enforces recognized typ value
mappings.notes: Existing test coverage in verify-local.test.ts

mappings.test_description: Warnings MUST be sorted by (pointer, code)
mappings.notes: Warning sort order verified in existing warning test suite

mappings.test_description: Warning code is REQUIRED on every VerificationWarning object
mappings.notes: All warning emissions include code field; property tests confirm structure

mappings.test_description: Warning message is REQUIRED on every VerificationWarning object
mappings.notes: All warning emissions include message field; property tests confirm structure

mappings.test_description: typ/peac_version coherence enforced after routing
mappings.notes: Property test: JOSE hardening applies regardless of strictness; coherence checked in crypto layer

mappings.test_description: Wire 0.1 route MUST NOT contain peac_version 0.2
mappings.notes: Crypto layer rejects typ=peac-receipt/0.1 with peac_version=0.2 (E_WIRE_VERSION_MISMATCH)

mappings.test_description: Wire 0.2 route MUST contain peac_version 0.2
mappings.notes: Crypto layer rejects typ=interaction-record+jwt without peac_version=0.2 (E_WIRE_VERSION_MISMATCH)

mappings.test_description: Challenge problem.type is REQUIRED (RFC 9457)
mappings.notes: Schema validation rejects challenge extension without problem.type; confirmed by fixture and property test

mappings.test_description: V2 offers must include maxTimeoutSeconds as a positive number
mappings.notes: verifyOfferV2 rejects zero and negative maxTimeoutSeconds

mappings.test_description: V2 verification rejects offers when supportedVersions excludes 2
mappings.notes: Default config rejects V2 offers

mappings.test_description: Unknown V2 shapes rejected in strict mode
mappings.notes: Missing required fields and malformed shapes produce errors

mappings.test_description: Default supportedVersions does not include 2
mappings.notes: V1 regression test confirms default config rejects V2

mappings.test_description: did:key resolves z (base58btc) multibase prefix
mappings.notes: Golden vector test with z-prefixed Ed25519 key

mappings.test_description: did:key resolves u (base64url) multibase prefix
mappings.notes: Golden vector test with u-prefixed Ed25519 key

mappings.test_description: did:key rejects non-Ed25519 keys without timing oracle
mappings.notes: Iterates all methods regardless of match

mappings.test_description: did:web transforms path segments to URL path components
mappings.notes: Path transformation per W3C CCG did:web method spec

mappings.test_description: did:web handles percent-encoded port in DID
mappings.notes: Port decoding from %3A to : in URL

mappings.test_description: did:web rejects IP literal hostnames
mappings.notes: IP rejection for SSRF protection

mappings.test_description: Resolved document id matches input DID exactly
mappings.notes: Exact id match enforcement

mappings.test_description: PKCE verifier is 43-128 chars from unreserved set
mappings.notes: validatePKCEVerifier rejects out-of-range and invalid chars

mappings.test_description: S256 is the only supported challenge method
mappings.notes: generatePKCEChallenge always returns method S256

mappings.test_description: Auth observation never produces allow or deny decision
mappings.notes: Decision immutability test across all auth methods

mappings.test_description: Token material never appears in auth evidence output
mappings.notes: Token omission test checks all token-shaped field names

mappings.test_description: gRPC carrier computes real SHA-256 receipt_ref
mappings.notes: Receipt ref is not a placeholder; matches sha256 pattern

mappings.test_description: gRPC carrier rejects binary metadata keys
mappings.notes: peac-receipt-bin key causes extract to return null

mappings.test_description: Default receipt type is interaction-record+jwt
mappings.notes: Wire 0.2 default verified in attach and addReceiptToMetadata

mappings.test_description: Resolver verifies sha256(jws) equals receipt_ref
mappings.notes: Carrier middleware delegates to verifyReceiptRef after fetch

mappings.test_description: Carrier middleware returns pure PeacEvidenceCarrier
mappings.notes: No _retrieval field on returned carrier

mappings.test_description: Resolver does not cache failed resolutions
mappings.notes: Second call after failure still calls resolveReceiptUrl

mappings.test_description: Non-strict mode returns carrier unchanged on failure
mappings.notes: Resolution failure, ref mismatch, and thrown errors all return original carrier

mappings.test_description: in-toto mapping uses first subject for source_ref
mappings.notes: Multi-subject test confirms first-subject-wins

mappings.test_description: SLSA mapping sets slsa.version to 1.2
mappings.notes: toPeacFromSlsa always produces version 1.2

mappings.test_description: in-toto mapping targets v1.0 Statement type only
mappings.notes: toPeacFromInToto throws for non-v1.0 _type

mappings.test_description: All runtime-governance records use evidence kind
mappings.notes: Round-trip issue+verify for each family asserts evidence kind via FAMILY_REGISTRY

mappings.test_description: Type URIs use org.peacprotocol/runtime-governance- prefix
mappings.notes: All type URIs start with TYPE_PREFIX assertion

mappings.test_description: Extension namespace is org.peacprotocol/runtime-governance
mappings.notes: EXTENSION_NAMESPACE equality assertion

mappings.test_description: Provider field is present in extension and never empty
mappings.notes: Extension field check asserts provider is present in decoded claims

mappings.test_description: Upstream integrity artifacts preserved as opaque strings
mappings.notes: Nested upstream cleanup test preserves source_system as opaque string

mappings.test_description: PEAC must not derive, recompute, rank, or authoritatively assess trust
mappings.notes: Trust score structural validation (0-1000 integer) without derivation or assessment

mappings.test_description: Compliance observations are observational, never authoritative determinations
mappings.notes: Compliance score structural validation (0-100) without compliance determination

mappings.test_description: validates and uses only allowed top-level keys
mappings.notes: 10 type URIs (Agent Card observation + 9 task/human events) covered by per-type happy-path validation; A2A_HANDOFF_TYPE_URIS.length === 10 asserted directly.

mappings.test_description: Task / human events: shape snapshot
mappings.notes: Each per-event schema literal-binds type to event via z.literal(); A2ATaskObservationSchema rejects type/event mismatch.

mappings.test_description: OpaqueRefSchema rejects grammar violations
mappings.notes: All *_ref fields use OpaqueRefSchema (no whitespace, no @, no JSON-opening character, recognized prefix); covered by 17 negative grammar tests + UTF-8 byte-length tests.

mappings.test_description: rejects legacy signature.verified shape
mappings.notes: Strict-object Agent Card schema rejects the legacy `signature` key; validateA2AHandoff returns stable error code a2a.legacy_signature_shape_blocked.

mappings.test_description: A2A observation helpers cannot import signature-verification APIs
mappings.notes: TS AST walker over import statements asserts no @peac/crypto, node:crypto, jose, or **/jws*/sign*/verify* imports in the 3 helper files.

mappings.test_description: validateA2AHandoff structured errors (Improvement 8)
mappings.notes: 20 forbidden top-level keys (decision/verdict/score/result/etc.) rejected with stable error a2a.observation_decision_blocked.

mappings.test_description: accepts discovery_path = ...
mappings.notes: Three discovery_path values exhaustively tested via it.each over the enum.

mappings.test_description: 9 events x happy-path produces a valid extension payload
mappings.notes: Each per-event schema literal-binds type to event via z.literal(); event/type mismatch rejected by discriminated union.

mappings.test_description: validateA2AHandoff structured errors
mappings.notes: RFC 3339 datetime with offset enforced via z.string().datetime({ offset: true }); malformed timestamps rejected with stable error a2a.timestamp_invalid.

mappings.test_description: rejects malformed upstream_event_digest
mappings.notes: upstream_event_digest validated by canonical Sha256DigestSchema (sha256:<64 lowercase hex>); malformed values rejected by zod parse.

mappings.test_description: minimal hashed observation accepted by CliExecutionSchema
mappings.notes: type literal CLI_COMMAND_EXECUTION_TYPE and surface.kind = 'cli' enforced as zod literals; positive fixture round-trips through safeParse.

mappings.test_description: command.program rejects path separators
mappings.notes: Top-level superRefine rejects '/' and '\\' in command.program with cli.schema_rejection. Builder also reduces user tokens to basename via platform-agnostic helper.

mappings.test_description: raw argv requires raw_capture_unsafely_allowed
mappings.notes: Cross-field superRefine emits cli.unsafe_flag_required when command.argv_mode='raw' but capture_policy.raw_capture_unsafely_allowed=false.

mappings.test_description: env entries must be subset of allowlist; mode/value discrimination
mappings.notes: Top-level superRefine emits cli.env_not_in_allowlist; env-block superRefine emits cli.env_mode_inconsistent for shape mismatches.

mappings.test_description: stream sample mutual exclusion + matched_pattern_category coupling + base64 canonical form + decoded length cap
mappings.notes: StreamRefSchema superRefine + top-level superRefine emit cli.stream_ref_inconsistent / cli.unsafe_flag_required; sample_base64 regex rejects non-canonical forms (length-mod-4 + alphabet).

mappings.test_description: shell_mode <-> shell_ref biconditional + path_mode != none + hashed-mode digest equivalence
mappings.notes: Top-level superRefine enforces all 4 invariants and emits cli.schema_rejection / cli.binary_path_mode_invalid.

mappings.test_description: rejects each of the 20 forbidden top-level keys with lifecycle.inline_value_blocked
mappings.notes: FORBIDDEN_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS asserted to have length 20; per-key it() iterates each value and asserts emitted error code lifecycle.inline_value_blocked with the offending key path.

mappings.test_description: rejects opaque-ref grammar violations (whitespace, JSON-leading, no-prefix, numeric strings, multilingual names)
mappings.notes: createOpaqueRefSchema with errorCode 'lifecycle.opaque_ref_grammar_violation' applied to every *_ref field; positive cases round-trip; negatives include numeric '0.92' result_ref, English/Devanagari/Han/Cyrillic name strings, JSON-opening payload, whitespace, and missing-prefix bare token.

mappings.test_description: approver_ref containing '@' rejects with lifecycle.approver_ref_pii_blocked (priority over general grammar code)
mappings.notes: ApproverRef Zod chain runs '@'-detection before LifecycleRef pipe; email-shape values surface the PII-blocked subclass; non-string approver_ref surfaces lifecycle.ref_must_be_string; whitespace/no-prefix surface general grammar code.

mappings.test_description: non-string *_ref values reject with lifecycle.ref_must_be_string and never leak generic Zod string-error text
mappings.notes: Number, object, array, boolean, null inputs emit only stable lifecycle.* codes; emitted message wraps the stable prefix; no Zod default 'Expected string' string surfaces as a public diagnostic.

mappings.test_description: per-event-kind required fields enforced via discriminated union (missing observed_at, missing approval_ref/approver_ref on approval-*, missing result_ref on evaluation_completed/experiment_result, etc.)
mappings.notes: Pre-flight enumerates required fields per event_kind discriminator and surfaces lifecycle.missing_required_field with the offending field path; missing observed_at uses the same code as other missing required fields, distinct from lifecycle.invalid_observed_at.

mappings.test_description: unknown event_kind rejects with lifecycle.event_kind_unknown
mappings.notes: Pre-flight resolves event_kind against EVENT_KINDS literal-set before Zod runs; foreign values surface lifecycle.event_kind_unknown rather than discriminated-union dispatch error.

mappings.test_description: malformed observed_at rejects with lifecycle.invalid_observed_at; missing observed_at uses lifecycle.missing_required_field (split confirmed)
mappings.notes: RFC 3339 z.string().datetime({ offset: true }) emits invalid_format mapped to lifecycle.invalid_observed_at when observed_at is present-but-malformed; absence flows through pre-flight as missing_required_field.

mappings.test_description: lifecycle records round-trip through @peac/protocol.issue() under each event_kind and verify under the caller-provided issuer key
mappings.notes: Positive case per event_kind asserts a 3-segment Wire 0.2 JWS; the JWS-payload-shape test decodes and asserts header.typ, payload.{peac_version,kind,type,iss}, the lifecycle extension key/value shape, and verify(jws, publicKey).valid === true.

mappings.test_description: every lifecycle type URI maps to org.peacprotocol/lifecycle-observation in TYPE_TO_EXTENSION_MAP
mappings.notes: Type-extension matrix iterates TYPE_TO_EXTENSION_MAP.entries() and asserts strict-mode match/missing/mismatch behavior; the lifecycle family supplies 9 entries all mapped to the lifecycle-observation extension key, and TYPE_TO_EXTENSION_MAP.size === 30 covers full registry composition.

mappings.test_description: spec orchestrator-boundary text is normative and vendor-neutral
mappings.notes: Section 8 of the profile spec carries the orchestrator boundary as normative prose with vendor-neutral nouns (orchestrators, workflow engines, evaluation systems, approval systems, agent runtimes); checked manually as part of spec review and pinned by repository search-and-replace discipline (no specific external project name appears in the normative §8 text).

mappings.test_description: rejects each of the 20 forbidden top-level credential-bearing keys with provisioning.inline_credential_blocked
mappings.notes: Uses internal PROVISIONING_FORBIDDEN_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS_INTERNAL to iterate every key; for each splices the key into a happy-path record and asserts the structured validator emits provisioning.inline_credential_blocked at the offending top-level path. The walker is depth-aware so the same key never emits both inline_credential_blocked and forbidden_key_name.

mappings.test_description: recursive walker rejects forbidden key names at any nesting depth past the top level, credential-shaped value strings, oversized strings, replacement characters, and structure caps; safeParse on the canonical schema enforces the same invariant
mappings.notes: Direct walker tests cover Bearer token values (token_material_blocked), env-assignment values (inline_credential_blocked), oversized strings (field_too_large), U+FFFD replacement characters (replacement_character_in_string), nested forbidden keys (forbidden_key_name), structure_too_deep on >32-level nesting, structure_too_large on >10K nodes. Generic regex categories only (jwt_compact / bearer_token / pem_private_key / env_assignment / connection_string_with_credentials); no vendor-shaped prefixes in the normative panel.

mappings.test_description: all *_ref fields use OpaqueRefSchema grammar; all *_digest fields use Sha256DigestSchema grammar
mappings.notes: Provider_ref / authorization_ref / issuer_ref / resource_ref / etc. are constructed via createOpaqueRefSchema with errorCode provisioning.opaque_ref_grammar_violation; positive shape tests use sha256:/urn:peac: forms; negatives ride through the *_ref Zod pipeline alongside the scheme_id grammar test (which exercises the same allow/reject grammar shape).

mappings.test_description: storage_surface object uses an abstract kind enum; vendor-specific values reject with provisioning.invalid_storage_surface
mappings.notes: StorageSurfaceKindSchema is a closed enum over external_secret_store / local_encrypted_file / local_plaintext_file / environment_file / runtime_secret_binding / none / unknown. The made_up_vault_kind negative case asserts provisioning.invalid_storage_surface. Vendor identity is carried via opaque provider_ref, never as a registered enum value.

mappings.test_description: scheme_id matches the bounded ASCII grammar (max 128 UTF-8 bytes; allowed [a-z0-9._:/+-]); scheme_id and scheme_ref are mutually exclusive
mappings.notes: SchemeIdSchema rejects whitespace via the grammar regex; tests assert provisioning.invalid_scheme_id on whitespace and on co-presence of scheme_id and scheme_ref.

mappings.test_description: max_amount_minor is a canonical non-negative bounded decimal-integer string: accepts `0` or `[1-9][0-9]*`; rejects leading-zero forms, decimals, exponent notation, signs/negative values, and empty strings, all with provisioning.invalid_amount_minor
mappings.notes: NonNegativeAmountMinorStringSchema wraps the existing AmountMinorStringSchema (byte cap + base-10 grammar) with a canonical-form refine() that accepts only `0` or `[1-9][0-9]*`. The canonical form prevents cross-language normalization ambiguity at this ceiling field. Tests cover `0` accept, `100` accept, `0001` reject (leading zero), `1.00` reject (decimal), `1e6` reject (exponent), `` reject (empty), `-100` reject (sign). All rejections surface provisioning.invalid_amount_minor via either the inner AmountMinorStringSchema regex/min(1) failure (path-dispatched in the validator) or the canonical-form refine() message.

mappings.test_description: per-event-kind required fields enforced via discriminated union; missing observed_at and other missing required fields surface provisioning.missing_required_field
mappings.notes: Pre-flight enumerates required fields per event_kind (catalog -> {catalog}; provider-link -> {provider}; account -> {provider, account}; resource -> {provider, resource}; credential -> {provider, credential}; payment-authorization -> {payment_authorization_observation}; budget -> {budget}; subscription -> {provider, subscription}; domain -> {domain}; deployment -> {deployment}); test asserts provisioning.missing_required_field on missing observed_at.

mappings.test_description: unknown top-level event_kind rejects with provisioning.invalid_event_kind; per-scope sub_event values reject with provisioning.invalid_sub_event; malformed observed_at/retrieved_at/expires_at/currency each surface their own stable codes (provisioning.invalid_observed_at / invalid_retrieved_at / invalid_expires_at / invalid_currency); benign unknown fields reject with provisioning.unrecognized_field rather than provisioning.inline_credential_blocked
mappings.notes: Pre-flight resolves the top-level event_kind against the closed 10-family literal-set before Zod runs; values not in the set surface provisioning.invalid_event_kind. Per-scope sub_event closed enums (account/resource/credential/subscription/domain/deployment/payment_authorization_observation) dispatch through path.endsWith('sub_event') -> provisioning.invalid_sub_event. Path-based dispatch on RFC-3339 fields ensures malformed observed_at -> invalid_observed_at, malformed catalog.retrieved_at -> invalid_retrieved_at, malformed expires_at -> invalid_expires_at, malformed currency -> invalid_currency, instead of collapsing into a single generic code. The unrecognized_keys Zod issue maps to provisioning.unrecognized_field so benign unknown top-level/nested fields do not get misclassified as credential leakage.

mappings.test_description: every PROVISIONING_LIFECYCLE_TYPE_URIS entry maps to org.peacprotocol/provisioning-lifecycle in TYPE_TO_EXTENSION_MAP; REGISTERED_EXTENSION_GROUP_KEYS includes the new namespace and size is 16
mappings.notes: The 10 type URIs and 1 extension group key are added to specs/kernel/registries.json; pnpm codegen:registries regenerates @peac/kernel/registries.generated.ts; the registry test asserts each URI maps to the new namespace and that the group set size is 16.

mappings.test_description: spec boundary text is normative and vendor-neutral; PEAC does not authorize the action, verify legal acceptance, provision resources, validate credentials, process payments, vouch for provider state, settle transactions, manage credential vaults, or operate the runtime; stale public-surface names from earlier revisions remain absent from the profile spec
mappings.notes: Section 3 of the profile spec carries the boundary paragraph as normative prose with vendor-neutral nouns. The tests/tooling/provisioning-lifecycle-profile-doc-truth.test.ts gate reads the spec at test time and asserts the boundary phrases are present (PEAC does not authorize the action; process payments; PEAC does not implement OAuth, DPoP, OAuth Protected Resource Metadata, or Shared Payment Tokens) and that older public-surface names (the prior recursive-walker name, the prior vendor-shaped pattern name, the legacy provider identity field) are absent. The literal stale strings are constructed at runtime via array .join() so they do not appear contiguously in the test source.

mappings.test_description: no-inline-content invariant: 20 forbidden top-level keys reject with agent.action.inline_content_blocked before Zod parse; FORBIDDEN_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS has 20 entries
mappings.notes: Pre-flight iterates AGENT_ACTION_FORBIDDEN_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS (prompt, message, messages, body, input, output, result, response, completion, stdout, stderr, env, secret, token, api_key, private_key, credential, model_output, tool_input, tool_output) and rejects each with agent.action.inline_content_blocked before the discriminated union parse runs.

mappings.test_description: opaque-ref grammar: *_ref fields must match the OpaqueRefSchema grammar (recognized prefix, no whitespace, no @, byte-bounded); violations surface agent.action.opaque_ref_grammar_violation
mappings.notes: agent_ref, action_ref, caller_ref, policy_ref, upstream_artifact_ref, parent_ref, delegated_to_ref, cancelled_by_ref all use createOpaqueRefSchema with errorCode agent.action.opaque_ref_grammar_violation and maxBytes 256. Tests cover: no-recognized-prefix, @ character, whitespace in urn:, numeric-only, plus did: and https: positive cases.

mappings.test_description: ref_must_be_string: non-string value for any *_ref field surfaces agent.action.ref_must_be_string
mappings.notes: Pre-flight 1.5 checks all known ref fields; numeric agent_ref, null action_ref, and object delegated_to_ref each surface agent.action.ref_must_be_string.

mappings.test_description: missing required fields: missing event_kind, agent_ref, action_ref, observed_at each surface agent.action.missing_required_field at the correct path
mappings.notes: Pre-flight 2 checks event_kind presence before value; pre-flight 3 checks observed_at presence; pre-flight 4 checks agent_ref and action_ref plus per-kind required fields (delegated_to_ref for delegated). Each missing field produces a separate error with the field name as path.

mappings.test_description: event_kind_unknown: unknown event_kind strings, empty string, numeric, and non-agent-action prefixes all reject with agent.action.event_kind_unknown
mappings.notes: Pre-flight 2 checks EVENT_KINDS closed enum (6 values). agent-action-executed-observed (valid-looking but wrong), empty string, 42 (numeric), lifecycle-approval-requested (wrong namespace) all surface event_kind_unknown.

mappings.test_description: invalid_observed_at: malformed observed_at and timeout_at surface agent.action.invalid_observed_at; missing observed_at surfaces missing_required_field not invalid_observed_at
mappings.notes: Malformed timestamps ('not-a-timestamp', '2026-05-14T10:00:00' without timezone) surface agent.action.invalid_observed_at. Missing observed_at emits missing_required_field via pre-flight 3. The missing/malformed split is enforced by the pre-flight ordering.

mappings.test_description: delegated: missing delegated_to_ref rejects with missing_required_field (per-kind required field enforcement)
mappings.notes: REQUIRED_BY_KIND maps agent-action-delegated-observed to ['delegated_to_ref']. Pre-flight 4 checks this per-kind list alongside the common required fields and surfaces agent.action.missing_required_field.

mappings.test_description: round-trip parity: all 6 positive vectors (one per event kind) are envelope-accepted with expected.accepted=true
mappings.notes: Six positive parity vectors (aa-001 through aa-006) cover invoked, delegated, approved, denied, cancelled, and timed-out event kinds. All assert expected.accepted=true. Negative vectors for semantic rejection live in the schema unit tests.

mappings.test_description: registry completeness: all 6 agent-action type URIs are registered in TYPE_TO_EXTENSION_MAP with AGENT_ACTION_EXTENSION_KEY; REGISTERED_EXTENSION_GROUP_KEYS includes org.peacprotocol/agent-action; no duplicate type URIs; validateAgentActionForType enforces type URI / event_kind agreement
mappings.notes: Registry mapping covered by dedicated agent-action-registry.test.ts: maps all 6 type URIs, checks REGISTERED_EXTENSION_GROUP_KEYS membership, asserts no duplicates. Type URI / event_kind enforcement covered by validateAgentActionForType tests in agent-action.test.ts (matching validates; mismatch emits type_event_kind_mismatch; unknown type URI emits type_uri_unknown).

mappings.test_description: orchestrator boundary (NORMATIVE): PEAC does not approve, deny, authorize, schedule, execute, govern, enforce, monitor, score, or orchestrate actions; the record describes what the caller observed, not what PEAC decided
mappings.notes: The boundary is enforced at the OBSERVER scope level: validateAgentAction accepts approved/denied event kinds as caller-reported observations; the schema has no fields for PEAC to issue decisions. The spec docs/specs/AGENT-ACTION-RECORDS.md Section 8 carries the normative boundary text.
